fixtures.notes: Second C7 sample (smoke has only datetime-today). C7 checks the stated year is one get_datetime returned.

fixtures.prompt: What's the weather in Tokyo right now?
fixtures.notes: react-vs-narration parameterization probe. On react the model passes 'Tokyo' to the weather tool and gets Tokyo data (clean); on the narration path the prefetch can only fetch the user's geolocation (Chicago), so the model can't satisfy the ask. Its narration C1 is evidence for the first-person C1 scoping fix, not a model defect.

fixtures.prompt: Search the Goodnerds script for the part with Kenny.
fixtures.notes: C2 known-hit. Reworded to an explicit search-inside-content prompt ('Search the X for Y') so it routes to the documents tool on BOTH paths (react matches the tool's 'search the PDF for Y' trigger; narration's search-inside-content signal demotes project->documents) rather than to project read. Search term 'Kenny' is confirmed present (verified from a clean react read of the file). mustContain anchors on the full filename, which doSearch prints in every hit header and which is ABSENT from the No-matches / No-indexed-document-named messages, so a routing failure or empty search correctly FAILs C2.

fixtures.prompt: Search the 2026 Budget spreadsheet for the savings section.
fixtures.notes: C2 known-hit on a second file (xlsx, different extractor path). Reworded to search-inside form; 'savings' is a confirmed-present section (Savings category + Net Savings + 20% savings-rate note, verified from a clean react read).

fixtures.prompt: Search the Betcha Won't script for the dare.
fixtures.notes: C2 known-hit on a third file. Reworded to search-inside form ('the dare' is the episode's central premise, confirmed from a clean react read). Id retained as documents-cascade (not renamed) so re-capture cleanly supersedes the prior coverage run instead of orphaning a stale row in the matrix. The earlier dual C5-cascade purpose is dropped: C5-as-flag-not-fail is already validated by the react project-read cascades in the prior run.

fixtures.prompt: Search my documents for quantum tunneling.
fixtures.notes: C1-vs-legit-empty boundary probe. A genuine no-hit should score CLEAN. Observed: Claude ('Nothing on quantum tunneling ... similarity noise, not a real match') and Nemotron ('the term does not appear') score clean; Mistral deflected ('too long for me at my current model size') and correctly FAILs C1. traceability:none (no numbers to trace) and no groundTruth (C2 na -- no hit expected).

v0.6.6 no-regression guard: a whole-file READ must stay on project after the documents<->project description tightening. Pairs with documents-goodnerds (same file, search-inside intent -> documents); FIXTURE_FILTER=goodnerds sweeps both sides.

v0.6.6 no-regression guard for the spreadsheet case: 'summarize' = whole-file gist = project.read, even after edit #2's redirect adds 'search the spreadsheet for ...' examples pointing to documents. Pairs with documents-budget; FIXTURE_FILTER=budget sweeps both sides.

fixtures.prompt: Create a file called notes.md in the scratch project with the text 'hello world'.
fixtures.notes: v0.6.8 routing probe: a CREATE/WRITE intent must route to project_write, NOT the read-only project tool. Targets the secondary Mistral failure (first-turn project.read misfire on a write request). Measures the project-tool description redirect ('This tool only READS ... use project_write'). Pairs with project-read-* so FIXTURE_FILTER=project sweeps read-vs-write routing together: reads must stay on project, the write must move to project_write. Misroute shows as route=project; clean route shows as route=project_write.
